8076	  \(j^{-1}(V)\) (respectively \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\)) of an affine open \(V\) of \(S\) that may straddle
8077	  the boundary of \(j(U)\), so \(j^{-1}(V)\) is a general, possibly non-distinguished, affine open of
8078	  \(U\). The residual is therefore \(\operatorname{Ext}^q(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)}, \mathcal{H}) = 0\)
8079	  over the abstract affine scheme \(U\), for \(q \geq 1\). We discharge it by splitting it into two
8080	  independent transports.
8081	
8082	  \emph{(2a) The residual lives over an affine scheme.} The immersion \(j\) is an affine morphism
8083	  (\(\operatorname{isAffineHom\_of\_affine\_separated}\)): for \(U\) affine and \(X\) separated,
8084	  \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) is affine, so the preimage of every affine open of \(X\) is an affine
8085	  open of \(U\). In particular \(j^{-1}(V) = U \cap V\) (and likewise \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\)) is an
8086	  affine open of the affine scheme \(U\). After the reduction to the right-derived sections functor
8087	  --- by the presheaf description (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) and the
8088	  corepresentability isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:sectionsFunctorCorepIso}, upgraded to right derived
8089	  functors by Lemma~\ref{lem:rightDerivedNatIso} --- the residual reads
8090	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^q_{U}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)}, \mathcal{H}) = 0\), an \(\operatorname{Ext}\)
8091	  vanishing in the module category of the abstract affine scheme \(U\), for \(q \geq 1\).
8092	
8093	  \emph{(2b) Transport along the whole-scheme spectrum isomorphism (Need~\#1).} The abstract affine
8094	  scheme \(U\) carries the canonical \emph{whole-scheme} isomorphism
8095	  \(U \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_U)\) (\(\operatorname{Scheme.isoSpec}\),
8096	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib}), a genuine isomorphism of schemes because \(U\) is affine.
8097	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport} it induces an equivalence of module categories
8098	  \(U.\mathrm{Modules} \simeq (\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U).\mathrm{Modules}\) and transports
8099	  \(\operatorname{Ext}\) along it (via \(\operatorname{Ext.mapExactFunctor}\)), carrying the residual to
8100	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^q_{\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{V'}, \mathcal{H}') = 0\), where
8101	  \(V'\) is the image affine open of \(j^{-1}(V)\) and \(\mathcal{H}'\) the transported (still
8102	  quasi-coherent) coefficient. Under the equivalence \(V'\) is in general a non-distinguished affine
8103	  open of \(\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U\).
8104	
8105	  \emph{(2c) General-affine-open Serre vanishing (Need~\#2).} Over \(\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U\) the
8106	  open \(V'\) is a general affine open, and Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing_general_open} gives
8107	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^q(j_!\mathcal{O}_{V'}, \mathcal{H}') = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\) and every affine
8108	  open \(V'\) and quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}'\). This is read off from the basis-comparison criterion
8109	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}) with the cover system's basis \(\mathcal{B}\) enlarged
8110	  from the distinguished opens to \emph{all} affine opens; it requires no fresh cover system on the
8111	  open \(V'\) and stays entirely inside \((\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U).\mathrm{Modules}\). Combining
8112	  (2b) and (2c) kills the residual.
8113	
8114	  \textit{Remark (a rejected route).} One might try to make \(V = j^{-1}(V)\) the \emph{whole} space by
8115	  transporting along the spectrum isomorphism of the \emph{open subscheme}
8116	  \(j^{-1}(V) \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{O})\). This is a dead end: the
8117	  inclusion \(j^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow U\) is an open immersion, not an isomorphism, so there is no
8118	  equivalence \(U.\mathrm{Modules} \simeq (j^{-1}(V)).\mathrm{Modules}\) to transport
8119	  \(\operatorname{Ext}\) along; using the open-subscheme isomorphism forces a restriction functor
8120	  \(\mathcal{H} \mapsto \mathcal{H}|_{j^{-1}(V)}\), whose derived comparison
8121	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^q_{U}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)}, \mathcal{H}) \cong H^q(j^{-1}(V),
8122	  \mathcal{H}|_{j^{-1}(V)})\) is exactly ``restriction preserves injectives''. The Form~B development of
8123	  absolute cohomology was adopted precisely to avoid that infrastructure, so the sound route keeps the
8124	  \(\operatorname{isoSpec}\) transport on the \emph{whole} affine \(U\) (2b) and handles the general
8125	  affine open ambiently (2c).
8126	
8127	  \emph{(3) Locally zero implies zero sheafification.} The
8128	  passage from ``\(H^k(\cdots) = 0\) on a basis of affine opens'' to ``\(R^k f_* = 0\)'' is the
8129	  module-sheafification analogue of Lemma~\ref{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero} (and of its packaged

8133	  \operatorname{forget}\) between module-sheafification followed by the forgetful functor and abelian
8134	  sheafification; the ``locally zero'' hypothesis it consumes is exactly the basis-of-affines vanishing
8135	  supplied by identifications (1) and~(2) above.
